patterns.title: Money — forecast / EVM / contract
patterns.cue: budget forward · CPI/SPI · EAC · vendor · unpaid
patterns.action: Update forecasts · EVM act · close contracts admin-complete
patterns.trap: Freeze BAC forever · overtime-only · skip admin close

patterns.title: OPA / lessons → actionable update
patterns.cue: estimation bias · vague policy · many changes delivered · lessons
patterns.action: Update OPA / templates / rewrite policy — actionable
patterns.trap: Archive-only / refer historical / interview without change

patterns.title: Change control / ICC
patterns.cue: out of baseline · unauthorized work · new feature request · CCB
patterns.action: Stop unauthorized if needed → impact → formal CR/CCB
patterns.trap: Absorb scope because 'value' · silent add · informal list

patterns.title: Adapt communications + culture
patterns.cue: preferred channel · multicultural · virtual · language
patterns.action: Adapt methods to prefs; culturally responsive protocols
patterns.trap: One-size email blast · ignore time zone/culture

patterns.title: Agile MVP / backlog / iteration protect
patterns.cue: MVP · mid-iteration · PO absent · backlog · sprint review
patterns.action: PO owns backlog · ship MVP · protect iteration · workshop AC
patterns.trap: Team absorbs mid-sprint · wait perfect · skip PO

patterns.title: Governance / roles / RAM / steering
patterns.cue: steering review · RACI/RAM · decision authority · unclear duty
patterns.action: Clarify roles (RAM) · prepare governance evidence · decision log
patterns.trap: Freestyle without authority map · skip steering prep

patterns.title: Risk cadence — register & response
patterns.cue: risk materialized · new law · risk in reports · reserve
patterns.action: Implement planned response · identify/assess · include in reports
patterns.trap: Invent new ritual · ignore register · lessons instead of act

patterns.title: Verify scope / acceptance trước 'done'
patterns.cue: sponsor confirm done · acceptance criteria · close check
patterns.action: Review scope & requirements / acceptance before claim success
patterns.trap: Declare done from timeline or team feeling alone

patterns.title: Tailor / hybrid — vừa đủ nghi thức
patterns.cue: small low-risk · mixed certainty · predictive+agile
patterns.action: Tailor weight · hybrid OK · adaptive estimates where uncertain
patterns.trap: Copy megaproject ceremony · or zero governance

patterns.title: Team stage / EI / work environment
patterns.cue: Tuckman · storming · shouting · noisy workspace · ownership
patterns.action: Map stage · EI/self-reg · PM owns environment · facilitate ownership
patterns.trap: Micromanage · escalate people issues early · ignore culture

patterns.title: Competency gap → develop
patterns.cue: interns · skill gap · training needs · onboarding
patterns.action: Gap analysis + develop/remediate (HR/mentor) — don't blame
patterns.trap: Fire / blame / ignore capability gap

patterns.title: Engagement plan — theo chiến lược SH
patterns.cue: resistant stakeholder · conflict interests · power/interest
patterns.action: Consult engagement plan; engage per documented strategy
patterns.trap: Freestyle escalate / ignore minority SH

patterns.title: Identify / re-analyze stakeholders
patterns.cue: new SH · stale distribution · who needs info now
patterns.action: Stakeholder analysis / update register before tweaking list alone
patterns.trap: Edit distribution list by gut without analysis

patterns.title: Quality — sampling / certification evidence
patterns.cue: mass production · quality supervisor · prove standards
patterns.action: Statistical sampling · independent certification test
patterns.trap: Inspect 100% when sampling fits · skip proof of quality

patterns.title: Transparency — tin xấu / bất định
patterns.cue: layoff · incomplete info · rumor · morale crash
patterns.action: Share known facts + facilitate open discussion
patterns.trap: Distract · promise certainty · silence · hide

patterns.title: Benefits / value metrics (not vanity)
patterns.cue: customer satisfaction · Kano · % value · long-term insight
patterns.action: Kano/surveys/benchmarking · measure value achieved · SH behavior insight
patterns.trap: Vanity SPI-only · ignore outcome metrics

patterns.title: Charter — authorize & align
patterns.cue: global HQ ops · need formal start · expectations
patterns.action: Create project charter to authorize and align
patterns.trap: Start work without charter · informal kickoff only

patterns.title: Coach conflict — không làm hộ
patterns.cue: privately struggling with teammate · interpersonal conflict
patterns.action: Coach conflict-resolution techniques; facilitate
patterns.trap: PM takes sides / punish / ignore / solve for them

patterns.title: Compliance / ethics — không exception
patterns.cue: regs · HQ vs local · ethics · audit · specialty law
patterns.action: Validate + escalate; never authorize ethics/regs exceptions
patterns.trap: Local exception / cut compliance for deadline / cover deviation

patterns.title: Knowledge transfer — outgoing / incoming
patterns.cue: turnover · transition · strict domain · KT sessions
patterns.action: Prioritize immediate knowledge transfer between people
patterns.trap: Delay KT · assume documentation enough under pressure

patterns.title: Own mistake — acknowledge + apologize
patterns.cue: wrong recipient email · unintended consequence · visible error
patterns.action: Acknowledge the mistake and apologize for consequences
patterns.trap: Hide · blame tool · over-explain without owning

patterns.title: Resilience — anticipate / respond / recover
patterns.cue: uncertainty · ambiguity · timeline+budget + SH concern
patterns.action: Build resilient approach (not 'eliminate all risk')
patterns.trap: Meeting-only · eliminate uncertainty claim

rowsPreview.stem: Stakeholders are frequently requesting modifications to the scope and priorities in response to evolving market feedback and competitive intelligence. Some teams prefer to revise plans at the local le
rowsPreview.correctLabel: C. Maintain the integrated plan as an evolving document and incorporate authorized updates to ensure ongoing alignment.

rowsPreview.stem: The project manager has been using story points to monitor progress. A burndown chart was created to present to stakeholders, highlighting the project’s performance over the past year. What month did
rowsPreview.correctLabel: D. December

rowsPreview.stem: A project manager at a mining company has been assigned the first engineering project since the company implemented a comprehensive new environmental policy. While integrating the required sustainabil
rowsPreview.correctLabel: A. Offset the cost of sustainability practices with the projected cost of failing regulatory compliance.

rowsPreview.stem: During testing, a software update shows several errors. The software development team double-checks to ensure they have followed the design documents properly. The sponsor pressures the team lead to d
rowsPreview.correctLabel: A. Engage subject matter experts (SMEs) to inspect the errors.

rowsPreview.stem: On a complex hardware prototype project, a cross-functional team reports a significant delay in producing the first prototype. The team lacks experience with a specialized testing tool, driving experi
rowsPreview.correctLabel: A,D,E

rowsPreview.stem: A project sponsor needs certainty that specific product features will be ready for mass production by a specific date. The project manager is evaluating agile development approaches to determine which
rowsPreview.correctLabel: B,D

rowsPreview.stem: A project manager is reviewing the team's performance dashboard from the last 30 days with the team and stakeholders. The project manager reports that the team has delivered the sprint predictably and
rowsPreview.correctLabel: A. Burndown for PI 2 Sprint 2 (by Story Points)

rowsPreview.stem: A collocated project team is facing communication issues. Some team members work early shifts (6 a.m. to 2 p.m.), others standard hours (9 a.m. to 5 p.m.), and a few work late (12 p.m. to 8 p.m.) due
rowsPreview.correctLabel: B. Schedule team meetings during the 2-hour overlap period with mandatory attendance.

rowsPreview.stem: During a daily coordination meeting, the project sponsor insists the project is on track, even though two milestones are already late. The clients stress that the continued setbacks may reduce their c
rowsPreview.correctLabel: A. Report any milestone delays to all stakeholders and explain the corrective actions taken to recover the schedule.

rowsPreview.stem: A new project manager joins a project in progress. Upon review, the project manager finds that there are no high-level quality standards documented for the project, and the team has been setting quali
rowsPreview.correctLabel: D. Revise the project charter to establish high-level quality standards that provide a consistent framework for all project deliverables.

rowsPreview.stem: An internal auditor finds several gaps in the company's adherence to quality processes. The project sponsor argues the gaps are minor and should not delay progress. The quality assurance manager insis
rowsPreview.correctLabel: D. Implement corrective actions immediately to ensure adherence to quality standards before resuming project work.

rowsPreview.stem: A project team is eager to begin working on the schedule, now that the project scope statement is complete. In a scope planning meeting, the functional managers tell the project manager that staff nee
rowsPreview.correctLabel: C. Use a bottom‑up WBS approach in collaboration with the team to capture all scope efficiently.

rowsPreview.stem: A public utility company needs to add generation and transmission infrastructure to serve its growing customer base. The project manager needs to evaluate the project to determine what is required for
rowsPreview.correctLabel: D. Create a project matrix showing project size, uncertainty, and complexity.

rowsPreview.stem: When the leadership team asked for an ad-hoc status update on a project’s current iteration, the project manager directed them to the project’s shared folder. After their review, the leadership team n
rowsPreview.correctLabel: D. Implemented a process to regularly update the project folder, ensuring artifacts are accurate and current.

rowsPreview.stem: A project manager is overseeing the implementation of a newly developed in-house software system intended to improve business processes. After deployment, adoption across the organization remains belo
rowsPreview.correctLabel: A,B,C

rowsPreview.stem: A project is nearing completion, and the customer proposes developing and publishing a business case to highlight the benefits delivered. The project manager and team are concerned that, at this stage
rowsPreview.correctLabel: B. Explain that creating a business case now might result in incomplete or misleading benefit information.

rowsPreview.stem: A project manager is leading an internal PMO improvement project that was originally sponsored by a PMO leader supportive of the initiative. Midway through execution, the sponsor is replaced, and the
rowsPreview.correctLabel: C. Share the tracked benefits and projected outcomes to illustrate the project’s value and relevance.

rowsPreview.stem: What should the project manager do to ensure that the expected project outcomes remain in alignment with the company's values?
rowsPreview.correctLabel: D. Ensure that all stakeholder concerns are understood and that they are guided through the changes resulting from the merger.

rowsPreview.stem: At a global engineering consultancy, a project manager is overseeing a multi-year infrastructure design program. Improvement actions are identified but treated as optional and adopted inconsistently.
rowsPreview.correctLabel: C. Integrate agreed improvement actions into standard practices and workflows so teams apply them consistently.

rowsPreview.stem: A project team created a new risk categorization matrix that improved the identification of systemic risks. The matrix is not yet part of the organization’s risk management framework. The project mana
rowsPreview.correctLabel: C. Capture the new risk matrix in the repository as a proposed organizational risk practice enhancement.

rows.stem: A company is initiating a new development project. The sponsor stresses that alignment between the project team and the stakeholders’ vision is critical for their success. Some stakeholders have raise
rows.correctLabel: D. Prepare a project charter to formalize initiation and align the team with stakeholders.

rows.stem: After distributing a project status report, the project manager discovers that several recipients are no longer involved with the project, while new stakeholders who should receive updates are not on
rows.correctLabel: A. Conduct stakeholder analysis to identify current information needs and requirements.

rows.stem: A project manager is creating an agenda and presentation materials for a kickoff meeting for a new global project. The project team spans multiple time zones, and some stakeholders have raised concern
rows.correctLabel: D. Coordinate with stakeholders to accommodate the teams in different time zones when scheduling the kickoff meeting.

rows.stem: A marketing firm is executing a multi-channel campaign with critical interdependencies. Midway through the project, poor communication between messaging and operations departments results in duplicati
rows.correctLabel: A. Establish governance checkpoints requiring escalation of unresolved interdepartmental communication issues.

rows.stem: A project manager for a new project is tasked with applying agile practices to improve team performance. The team has mixed experience with agile methodologies, and stakeholders expect quick adoption
rows.correctLabel: A. Assess the team's current agile maturity level and develop a targeted training plan based on identified gaps.

rows.stem: A project is nearly complete, and a project manager learns that a key stakeholder is leaving the organization. The departing stakeholder holds important context about project decisions, acceptance cri
rows.correctLabel: A. Update the stakeholder register and stakeholder engagement plan to reflect the change and identify how engagement will continue.

rows.stem: A project manager is initiating a public-facing service upgrade with multiple regulatory touchpoints. The sponsor expresses concern about potential expectation gaps that may surface later in the proje
rows.correctLabel: A. Develop a stakeholder register to classify stakeholders, and the RACI (responsible, accountable, consulted, informed) matrix to prioritize actions.

rows.stem: Team members assigned to a complex project are having trouble understanding their specific duties and their roles within the project. As a result, they have not been working together as effectively as
rows.correctLabel: B. Create a responsibility assignment matrix (RAM) to identify which team member is responsible for each task.

rows.stem: An agile project to implement a new automation process in a factory is underway. The project manager has selected a specialized vendor to support the technical installation phase. The vendor team will
rows.correctLabel: B. Hold orientation meetings to ensure both parties share understanding of project goals and requirements.

rows.stem: During a standup meeting, the project manager discovers that one of the software developers is struggling to complete work because they lack experience with automation testing, potentially affecting t
rows.correctLabel: C. Ask the quality assurance engineer to assist the software developer as soon as possible.

rows.stem: An urban hotel design project involves a globally distributed, multidisciplinary team with diverse cultural backgrounds, working across multiple time zones. The company has previously experienced dela
rows.correctLabel: C. Develop a culturally responsive communication protocol that defines channels, escalation flows, and language needs.

rows.stem: Midway through a project, the project manager notices that team members are disengaged and not collaborating effectively. What should the project manager have done during the early stages of the proje
rows.correctLabel: A. Conducted an assessment of team dynamics.

rows.stem: A project manager is leading a new implementation project involving team members located across several countries and time zones. The project manager needs to establish an effective team engagement ap
rows.correctLabel: A. Create a communication plan tailored to team availability and location.

rows.stem: In a predictive project, the communications management plan requires that all stakeholder updates be reviewed by the project manager before being sent. Midway through execution, a functional team lead
rows.correctLabel: A. Address the team leader privately to review the process, explain the impact, and agree on compliance steps.

rows.stem: A project manager is leading a high-visibility innovation project that has strong executive sponsorship and is under tight deadlines. A recent requirement change mandates the use of a new and emerging
rows.correctLabel: A. Reassign the team member to the task to support their growth at the organization, and acquire a resource to replace the team member's old role.

rows.stem: In the early stages of team formation on a high-visibility project, the project manager is struggling to focus on strategic planning and stakeholder engagement because team members frequently request
rows.correctLabel: A. Encourage the team to resolve conflicts independently and build accountability.

rows.stem: A new project involves team members from two countries with minimal time zone overlap - only one hour when both sites are working. Cultural differences affect communication styles, with one site prefe
rows.correctLabel: A. Establish structured asynchronous communication protocols with formal handoff procedures.

rows.stem: A project manager observes that a key team member frequently misses team meetings and prioritizes work assigned by their functional manager. This misalignment has begun to affect both delivery timelin
rows.correctLabel: A. Escalate the conflict to the functional manager and renegotiate the team member’s availability.

rows.stem: A solar energy company needs to adapt to an energy market marked by rapid technological shifts, government incentives, and policy uncertainty. The sponsor stresses that strategic decisions must delive
rows.correctLabel: A. Collaborate with internal stakeholders to define and prioritize the value that components' outcomes should deliver.

rows.stem: Stakeholders are frequently requesting modifications to the scope and priorities in response to evolving market feedback and competitive intelligence. Some teams prefer to revise plans at the local le
rows.correctLabel: C. Maintain the integrated plan as an evolving document and incorporate authorized updates to ensure ongoing alignment.

rows.stem: Based on lessons learned, what actions should the project manager take to implement continuous improvement for the team?
rows.correctLabel: D. Explore how the tool reduced time spent on administrative tasks and apply those methods to other areas.

rows.stem: The project manager has been using story points to monitor progress. A burndown chart was created to present to stakeholders, highlighting the project’s performance over the past year. What month did
rows.correctLabel: D. December

rows.stem: As the launch window approaches, sponsors are demanding visible progress. Delivery options include an early limited release, delaying for more features, or sticking to the original plan despite readin
rows.correctLabel: B. Define a limited release option by clearly stating its value objectives, acceptance criteria, and risk boundaries.

rows.stem: A global project pays suppliers in three currencies. As a result of sudden shifts in exchange rates, projected project costs rise by 10%. The project sponsor pressures the project manager to absorb th
rows.correctLabel: D. Absorb exchange rate losses fully through contingency funds, even if reserves are quickly depleted.

rows.stem: A multinational enterprise is bringing a new product to market. As the project nears completion, the project manager is notified of a disagreement among end users regarding the functionality of a spec
rows.correctLabel: C,D

rows.stem: A project manager at a mining company has been assigned the first engineering project since the company implemented a comprehensive new environmental policy. While integrating the required sustainabil
rows.correctLabel: A. Offset the cost of sustainability practices with the projected cost of failing regulatory compliance.

rows.stem: During testing, a software update shows several errors. The software development team double-checks to ensure they have followed the design documents properly. The sponsor pressures the team lead to d
rows.correctLabel: A. Engage subject matter experts (SMEs) to inspect the errors.

rows.stem: An energy company is investing in a new power plant, and the project team members have been instructed to make sustainability a key focus in their designs. The project manager needs to ensure that the
rows.correctLabel: B. Analyze the sustainability strategy to identify relevant processes and practices that are already integrated into enterprise environmental factors (EEFs).

rows.stem: Stakeholders are planning a clearly defined project with several components and a limited budget. The project manager is creating a procurement strategy and is in the process of selecting vendors. How
rows.correctLabel: D. Use a fixed-budget method to disclose the available funds to the invited sellers and select the highest-ranking technical proposal within budget.

rows.stem: A years-long project with repeated technological failures is closing. A similar project is planned in a disaster-prone region. Stakeholders have asked the project manager to ensure lessons learned are
rows.correctLabel: C. Facilitate lessons learned workshops and process assets with an updated risk breakdown structure (RBS).

rows.stem: On a complex hardware prototype project, a cross-functional team reports a significant delay in producing the first prototype. The team lacks experience with a specialized testing tool, driving experi
rows.correctLabel: A,D,E

rows.stem: A project sponsor needs certainty that specific product features will be ready for mass production by a specific date. The project manager is evaluating agile development approaches to determine which
rows.correctLabel: B,D

rows.stem: A project manager is reviewing the team's performance dashboard from the last 30 days with the team and stakeholders. The project manager reports that the team has delivered the sprint predictably and
rows.correctLabel: A. Burndown for PI 2 Sprint 2 (by Story Points)

rows.stem: A project is being implemented in a company. The research and development department has identified new features they want added to the project's deliverables during the execution phase. What should t
rows.correctLabel: C. Ask the research and development department to submit a change request through the formal change control process.

rows.stem: A collocated project team is facing communication issues. Some team members work early shifts (6 a.m. to 2 p.m.), others standard hours (9 a.m. to 5 p.m.), and a few work late (12 p.m. to 8 p.m.) due
rows.correctLabel: B. Schedule team meetings during the 2-hour overlap period with mandatory attendance.

rows.stem: A project manager is leading an initiative to implement a new customer relationship management platform. The planning team has just been assembled, and stakeholders have been meeting to brainstorm pla
rows.correctLabel: C. Develop the project scope and responsibility assignment matrix (RAM) after approval of the charter.

rows.stem: Project team members have been assigned to a project in the planning phase. Vendors and external partners involved in the project have varying levels of awareness about project timelines, roles, and c
rows.correctLabel: B. Rely on the communications management plan to properly inform all stakeholders, including vendors and external partners.

rows.stem: An engineering project is moving from the development phase to production. The project manager has a closing checklist for this phase that covers all the standard tasks. However, product development i
rows.correctLabel: B. Identify outstanding risks that should be accepted or transitioned to the production team.

rows.stem: An international space project requires a project team composed of teams in four different countries. The project manager needs to identify which project planning document governs how team members acr
rows.correctLabel: B. Reference the resource management plan to understand how team members across locations will be assigned and coordinated.

rows.stem: During the planning phase of a project, the project manager is struggling to identify key stakeholders. Without the right stakeholders, gathering accurate requirements and securing user feedback will
rows.correctLabel: C. Refer to the project charter to identify stakeholders named during project initiation.

rows.stem: During a daily coordination meeting, the project sponsor insists the project is on track, even though two milestones are already late. The clients stress that the continued setbacks may reduce their c
rows.correctLabel: A. Report any milestone delays to all stakeholders and explain the corrective actions taken to recover the schedule.

rows.stem: A project manager implemented an approved change to manage a risk in a project. The change was successful, and the project is nearing closure. The project manager wants to ensure that the knowledge ga
rows.correctLabel: D. Update the lessons learned register with details about the risk, the implemented change, and the outcome.

rows.stem: A construction project is underway. The project manager is concerned about discrepancies between the minor milestones outlined in the project management plan and the order in which the vendor is actua
rows.correctLabel: B. Adjust the project plan to align with the vendor’s work approach and integrate it with the vendor’s perspective and work outputs.

rows.stem: A project deliverable does not meet the agreed-upon quality specifications and the vendor is in dispute over whether the defects are their responsibility. The project manager must identify which docum
rows.correctLabel: C. Reference the contract agreements to determine the vendor's obligations, warranties, and liability for defective deliverables.

rows.stem: A new project manager is hired and assigned to a software development project using agile practices. The manager estimates the timeline based on experience from a previous organization. However, this
rows.correctLabel: D. Ask the team to estimate story points and monitor their velocity over time.

rows.stem: A project manager has been assigned to a project that requires resources with a wide range of expertise and skills from multiple business units within and outside the organization. The project manager
rows.correctLabel: C. Create a responsibility assignment matrix (RAM) to define and document roles and responsibilities.

rows.stem: After five iterations of an agile project to develop a new product, user stories have been completed and sprint goals achieved. Before formally closing the agile project, the team must complete two sp
rows.correctLabel: C. Facilitate an updated analysis of the business and market for the product, and process customer feedback through iteration demos and user tests.

rows.stem: A large construction company is in the early stages of a hybrid project that combines predictive infrastructure work with adaptive software development. Stakeholders are complaining that the early pha
rows.correctLabel: A. Reevaluate sequencing with stakeholders to assess earlier delivery of high-value features without compromising stability.

rows.stem: A project manager is planning a complex project that must deliver a high-quality product within a set budget. Stakeholders are pressuring for early delivery due to competitive market pressures, but th
rows.correctLabel: A. Use three-point estimation, incorporating optimistic, pessimistic, and most likely scenarios.

rows.stem: A project team is discussing an item in the backlog during estimating. The team realizes they lack sufficient technical knowledge to accurately estimate the effort required for a complex, unfamiliar f
rows.correctLabel: B. Add a new spike item to the product backlog to investigate the technical unknowns before estimating the original item.

rows.stem: A steering committee is applying the weighted scoring model to decide which project to pursue next. The committee ranked Project 2 as the top candidate. However, after reviewing the results, the proje
rows.correctLabel: C. Recommend Project 2 because it ranked highest according to the agreed-upon scoring model unless management formally revises the criteria.

rows.stem: In the middle of a project, the project manager realizes that a specific set of skills is needed to complete project development. Unfortunately, none of the team members possess the necessary skills.
rows.correctLabel: B. Review the timeline and budget to consider training, outsourcing, or other alternatives.

rows.stem: A new project manager joins a project in progress. Upon review, the project manager finds that there are no high-level quality standards documented for the project, and the team has been setting quali
rows.correctLabel: D. Revise the project charter to establish high-level quality standards that provide a consistent framework for all project deliverables.

rows.stem: During the development of the work breakdown structure (WBS), the project manager is asked by the sponsor to demonstrate how the defined scope will be directly tied to both the budget and the schedule
rows.correctLabel: A. Establish control accounts that integrate scope with budget and schedule.

rows.stem: A project manager is negotiating a contract with a first-time vendor that will be involved in an upcoming project. The vendor is reluctant to sign until they are convinced that the terms of the agreem
rows.correctLabel: B. Incorporate data-driven insights from historical records to guide the negotiations.

rows.stem: A client brings a project manager into a project during the execution stage. Upon reviewing the work breakdown structure, the project manager discovers several deliverables that appear to be missing f
rows.correctLabel: B. Verify with stakeholders whether the omissions were intentional, include the missing work in the WBS, recalculate costs, and initiate a change request.

rows.stem: An internal auditor finds several gaps in the company's adherence to quality processes. The project sponsor argues the gaps are minor and should not delay progress. The quality assurance manager insis
rows.correctLabel: D. Implement corrective actions immediately to ensure adherence to quality standards before resuming project work.

rows.stem: User test results for a prototype release are ready. Some test users want smoother transitions and better readability, while others request enhanced functionality that was not part of the original pro
rows.correctLabel: B. Conduct further testing and recommend the validated feedback to the product owner for consideration in updating the product backlog.

rows.stem: During inspection, the project manager identifies defects in the delivered equipment. The vendor has reviewed the shipment and insists the items meet the contract specifications. The project sponsor u
rows.correctLabel: C. Trigger the contract dispute resolution process and engage all parties in a formal review.

rows.stem: A product manufacturer is developing a new product. The project sponsor proposes reducing the number of inspection steps due to budget pressure. The quality assurance manager warns that this could pot
rows.correctLabel: D. Retain full inspections to preserve quality, explaining the trade-offs clearly to the stakeholders.

rows.stem: A hybrid project with multiple teams and vendors is executing an integrated management plan. Several teams propose local plan changes in response to risks, feedback, and improvements. Individually, ch
rows.correctLabel: B. Review all proposed adjustments together for overall impacts.

rows.stem: A software development company is developing a new revision for an existing product. During system integration, two functional managers dispute ownership of a major interface. The requirements documen
rows.correctLabel: A. Clarify ownership by updating the WBS dictionary and realigning responsibilities with stakeholder agreement.

rows.stem: A project team is eager to begin working on the schedule, now that the project scope statement is complete. In a scope planning meeting, the functional managers tell the project manager that staff nee
rows.correctLabel: C. Use a bottom‑up WBS approach in collaboration with the team to capture all scope efficiently.

rows.stem: A public utility company needs to add generation and transmission infrastructure to serve its growing customer base. The project manager needs to evaluate the project to determine what is required for
rows.correctLabel: D. Create a project matrix showing project size, uncertainty, and complexity.

rows.stem: A project manager is assigned to a project similar in scope and complexity to a previous initiative. The sponsor explains that in the earlier project, the costs of several tasks were underestimated an
rows.correctLabel: C. Analyze lessons learned from the previous project to quantify impacts and determine corrective responses.

rows.stem: A project manager is alerted by a leading indicator to a potential problem. The speed with which the team is meeting milestones in one workflow has slowed. The cause is not yet clear, and the project
rows.correctLabel: C. Consult the communications management plan for guidance on who should be notified and when.

rows.stem: A pharmaceutical company has developed a product with known risks. Results from a clinical trial reveals significant adverse effects of health consequences among users. The project sponsor insists on
rows.correctLabel: D. Communicate with key stakeholders regarding early closure plans and expectations.

rows.stem: An environmental monitoring equipment manufacturer has initiated a project to develop a new diagnostic unit and is now midway through the execution phase. The team has completed assembly of the first
rows.correctLabel: D. Use inspections and statistical sampling to assess the defect and confirm compliance with quality requirements.

rows.stem: A radar device manufacturer has revised their popular product and is getting ready to deliver it to a new customer. Before the product is shipped, the governing regulatory body issues new guidance for
rows.correctLabel: B. Submit a formal change request to integrate the new regulatory requirements into the approved project plan before delivery.

rows.stem: A project manager is working on launching a minimum viable product (MVP) for a new-to-market product. The team has successfully progressed to the third iteration out of a planned total of six. During
rows.correctLabel: D. Collaborate with stakeholders to redefine MVP scope and adjust the release plan to launch earlier.

rows.stem: When the leadership team asked for an ad-hoc status update on a project’s current iteration, the project manager directed them to the project’s shared folder. After their review, the leadership team n
rows.correctLabel: D. Implemented a process to regularly update the project folder, ensuring artifacts are accurate and current.

rows.stem: The project sponsor is increasingly concerned about the value being delivered and wants assurance that the project is staying on schedule and aligned with expected benefits. What should the project ma
rows.correctLabel: C. Compare current performance against previous forecasts.

rows.stem: A healthcare organization is in mid-project execution of their annual patient database system update when a key stakeholder raises concerns about new regulations regarding patient privacy protection.
rows.correctLabel: B. Compare estimated versus actual impacts on scope, cost, and schedule deliverables to quantify benefits.

rows.stem: Halfway through a project, a vendor reassigns key engineers to another project, leaving gaps in delivery capacity. The client is concerned about potential delays. The sponsor argues it is the vendor’s
rows.correctLabel: B. Enforce the contract clause requiring named resources and demand immediate corrective staffing action.

rows.stem: A vendor submits a final invoice at project closure. The client refuses to pay the invoice, citing unresolved defects. The sponsor insists on immediate payment to close accounts, but the procurement t
rows.correctLabel: A. Withhold payment until deliverables are formally verified against all contractual obligations.

rows.stem: A major event planned for tens of thousands of people is fast approaching. The project manager discovers financial reports that show a primary vendor is close to bankruptcy. The sponsor urges reducing
rows.correctLabel: C. Initiate contingency planning with the procurement manager and explore alternative supplier options.

rows.stem: At the beginning of a project, the project manager meets with the product owner and the business analyst to work on a requirements-gathering process, creating a list of requirements, features, and the
rows.correctLabel: A,E

rows.stem: Critical project equipment from an overseas vendor fails quality surveys, showing noncompliance with key specifications that could impact project deliverables. The vendor claims specifications were un
rows.correctLabel: A. Conduct a comprehensive impact analysis of noncompliance with project objectives and quality standards.

rows.stem: During a final inspection of a new product, the project team discovers a significant defect in one of the key deliverables. This defect poses a security risk to the client, who expects to start using
rows.correctLabel: A. Conduct a quality investigation and initiate corrective action.

rows.stem: A project manager is overseeing the delivery of a new enterprise software system. During a stakeholder review session, a key stakeholder reports that a critical component of the product is “not user-f
rows.correctLabel: B. Collaborate with the stakeholder to clarify the issue, verify a solution, and update acceptance criteria if necessary.

rows.stem: The service provider and client have agreed on about 80% of the project scope for the delivery of marketing content. The client has a fixed budget and completion date, but wants to maximize business v
rows.correctLabel: C. Propose a flexible scope contract with a fixed number of iterations to manage budget and time.

rows.stem: The project manager is assigned to a new, high-visibility initiative with a fixed budget approval deadline. The project is similar in scope and complexity to one completed by the organization three ye
rows.correctLabel: B. Apply the previous project’s total cost, adjusted using a standard escalation factor per company policy.

rows.stem: A company in a highly regulated industry is preparing to launch a new, first-of-its-kind product. To prevent competitors from entering the market first, the CEO asks the project manager to recommend a
rows.correctLabel: C. Adopt a hybrid approach with early iterations for development and predictive tasks scheduled just before rollout.

rows.stem: A project manager is overseeing an agile project that has finalized scope and compliance requirements. During a regular project review, a stakeholder informs the project manager that proposed changes
rows.correctLabel: B. Analyze the impact of the legislative changes on the project plan.

rows.stem: A lengthy upgrade project is being delivered in a series of phased outputs. The end-users will face some level of disruption during each phase prior to achieving the outputs. What should the project m
rows.correctLabel: D. Monitor phase outcomes against planned value indicators and stakeholder expectations.

rows.stem: A project manager has just taken over a 5-year project that is at the midpoint. At the steering committee meeting, the CEO asked whether the project was still on track to meet its business case goal a
rows.correctLabel: C. Met with the project sponsor to review business case alignment and the status of deliverables.

rows.stem: A project manager is overseeing the implementation of a newly developed in-house software system intended to improve business processes. After deployment, adoption across the organization remains belo
rows.correctLabel: A,B,C

rows.stem: A project is nearing completion, and the customer proposes developing and publishing a business case to highlight the benefits delivered. The project manager and team are concerned that, at this stage
rows.correctLabel: B. Explain that creating a business case now might result in incomplete or misleading benefit information.

rows.stem: A project manager is leading an internal PMO improvement project that was originally sponsored by a PMO leader supportive of the initiative. Midway through execution, the sponsor is replaced, and the
rows.correctLabel: C. Share the tracked benefits and projected outcomes to illustrate the project’s value and relevance.

rows.stem: A multinational company introduces a sustainability reporting system to comply with new regulations, but a regional office customizes reports independently, leading to inconsistencies. Nonstandard rep
rows.correctLabel: A. Escalate the issue via compliance and governance channels to set standards and clarify allowed differences.

rows.stem: In a construction project, the project manager has identified two independent cost threats: Risk 1: 30% probability, $100,000 impact if it occurs. Risk 2: 20% probability, $250,000 impact if it occurs

Ongoing regulatory updates and stakeholder input are resulting in multiple change requests that impact delivery timelines, risks, and resource allocation. Senior leadership is concerned about maintain
rows.correctLabel: C. Consolidate proposed changes and assess their cumulative impact before presenting options.

rows.stem: What can the project manager do to assess each organization's culture and help team members embrace the inevitable changes resulting from the merger?
rows.correctLabel: D. Consider the unique characteristics of each team’s culture and tailor communication to maximize engagement.

rows.stem: What should the project manager do to ensure that the expected project outcomes remain in alignment with the company's values?
rows.correctLabel: D. Ensure that all stakeholder concerns are understood and that they are guided through the changes resulting from the merger.

rows.stem: At a global engineering consultancy, a project manager is overseeing a multi-year infrastructure design program. Improvement actions are identified but treated as optional and adopted inconsistently.
rows.correctLabel: C. Integrate agreed improvement actions into standard practices and workflows so teams apply them consistently.

rows.stem: A project team created a new risk categorization matrix that improved the identification of systemic risks. The matrix is not yet part of the organization’s risk management framework. The project mana
rows.correctLabel: C. Capture the new risk matrix in the repository as a proposed organizational risk practice enhancement.

rows.stem: A project manager is meeting with key stakeholders to initiate departmental reorganization. The sponsor is concerned that this might impact timely oversight and decision-making as staff adjust to thei
rows.correctLabel: D. Create a responsibility assignment matrix and an organizational hierarchical chart.

rows.stem: A project team conducted a retrospective to identify lessons learned on a recent project. The results have been recorded in the lessons learned registry. The project manager is determined to not let t
rows.correctLabel: D. Use storytelling to make the lessons learned more relatable and memorable.

rows.stem: A small company has been acquired by a larger company. A project manager has been tasked with leading the marketing teams through the process of dealing with the organizational change resulting from t
rows.correctLabel: D. Identify the nature and scope of change, whether it is a shift in strategy, new leadership, or policy change.

rows.stem: The sponsor directs the project manager to use performance data collected over several months to establish a culture of continuous improvement. The project manager is tasked with identifying weaknesse
rows.correctLabel: B. Integrate the plan-do-check-act (PDCA) cycle into workflows to embed continuous project improvement.

rows.stem: A marketing company is undertaking a project to roll out a new internal technology system. The project manager has been tasked with identifying the best product for the organization and tailoring its
rows.correctLabel: A. Examine the organizational culture to align the selection with business outcomes and identify the values that drive organizational change.

rows.stem: A public university is investing a portion of its funding in an innovative construction project. The project sponsor wants to ensure that the risks associated with this investment are evaluated regula
rows.correctLabel: A. Implement investment controls to evaluate and analyze the possible returns on investment and reduce potential risks.

rows.stem: Several senior partners at an architecture firm are preparing for retirement. These partners possess institutional knowledge and expertise that the firm needs to retain after the partners leave. To pr
rows.correctLabel: D. Document lessons learned from each project phase and ensure the organization's information repositories are kept current.

rows.stem: An organization is planning a marketing outreach initiative. The project manager is working with the project sponsor, the marketing manager, and the project management office (PMO) to ensure alignment
rows.correctLabel: C. Consult with the PMO for guidance on project governance, including issue and risk management, to develop the required framework.

rows.stem: A national restaurant chain is facing competition from a new startup with franchise locations across multiple cities. Project stakeholders have assigned a project manager to address complex industry i
rows.correctLabel: C,E

rows.stem: During a daily coordination meeting, a team member expresses the belief that the company does not promote fairly from within the organization. Another team member states that management does not do en
rows.correctLabel: A. Review artifacts for aspects of the organization's culture and for a possible cultural disconnect that needs to be addressed.

rows.stem: An engineering firm was awarded a large government contract. During project execution, the project manager learns of a new feature that the government agency would prefer over the one specified in the
rows.correctLabel: B. Execute a change request and submit it to the change control board so the impact of the change can be reviewed before acceptance or rejection.

rows.stem: A pharmaceutical project includes an agile team of doctors who analyze human tissue samples and generate large volumes of complex biological data. Advances in machine learning have made it possible to
rows.correctLabel: D. Work with the team to plan how they can leverage machine learning capabilities based on the data they are already generating.

rows.stem: A banking company is designing a new user interface for its customers. The project manager needs to monitor progress and ensure that all requirements are tracked and met. To do so, the project manager
rows.correctLabel: B. Change the priority of “User must be able to view balance” to High and set the priority of “System must support multi-factor authentication (MFA)” to Highest.

rows.stem: A project team is using three-point estimates for several activities. The pessimistic values are much higher than expected, which makes the project sponsor nervous about sharing the schedule with seni
rows.correctLabel: D. Retain the pessimistic values because they highlight risk exposure and are essential for contingency planning.

rows.stem: A large medical office complex is facing staffing and personnel management challenges. Recruitment issues, inadequate training, and high staff turnover are causing costly disruptions to office operati
rows.correctLabel: B. Focus on filling critical staff roles and reducing personnel turnover to ensure safe and effective patient care.

rows.stem: An internal auditor discovers that a developer has been working on a feature not included in the baseline. The project sponsor argues that the feature adds value and should be retained. The compliance
rows.correctLabel: A. Stop the unauthorized work and initiate a change control request.

rows.stem: Over the past 6 months, the development cost per user story has risen by more than 250%. The product owner and project manager need to determine whether continuing the current development trajectory r
rows.correctLabel: C. Run a cost-benefit analysis with the product owner to evaluate whether the value being delivered justifies the increasing development costs.

rows.stem: A multinational project team faces repeated miscommunications around cultural differences. Prior projects documented similar challenges, but the improvement framework does not address cross‑cultural c
rows.correctLabel: D. Request a corresponding cross‑cultural practices change to the organization’s training program.

rows.stem: A local radio station has been bought by a larger syndicate that will adjust daily operations. The parent company has hired a project manager to evaluate the impact of the acquisition. The project man
rows.correctLabel: D. Perform a change impact assessment (CIA) and then execute an impact matrix.

rows.stem: A project manager is receiving numerous change requests for various corrective actions, preventative actions, defect repairs, and updates, long after a project’s baselines have been established. The p
rows.correctLabel: B. Determine whether the requested change is likely to impact one of the project baselines.

rows.stem: A project is operating on schedule under an approved set of assumptions. The project manager is informed of a major organizational restructuring. This change will not end the project but will constrai
rows.correctLabel: A. Update the communications management plan with a strategy for conveying this change to the stakeholders.

rows.stem: A project manager working on a project dependent on federal grant funding learns that funding will be reduced next year. Additionally, several significant regulatory changes are expected to become law
rows.correctLabel: C. Establish a regular cadence to assess project impact and make adjustments as changes arise.

rows.stem: At the start of a new healthcare IT project, the project manager reviews the lessons learned repository. Several past projects highlight recurring issues with regulatory compliance documentation. How
rows.correctLabel: D. Request a change to the procurement management plan’s compliance checklists using repository insights.

rows.stem: A regulated, multi-supplier project is approaching a governance checkpoint to authorize the next phase, which will lock in supplier commitments and reduce flexibility. Several medium risks in data sec
rows.correctLabel: B. Present continuation options framed by aggregated risk exposure and remaining response flexibility.

rows.stem: Match each definition to the correct AI concept (Machine learning (ML), Deep learning (DL), Generative AI (GenAI), Artificial intelligence (AI)). Drag and drop the term below to the correct definition
rows.correctLabel: D. Artificial intelligence (AI) · C. Generative AI (GenAI) · A. Machine learning (ML) · B. Deep learning (DL) (theo thứ tự mô tả 1→4)

rows.stem: During a mid‑project audit, the quality team discovers that recurring vendor delays were reported in lessons learned from earlier projects. The same vendor practices are now causing repeated schedule
rows.correctLabel: C. Request a change to the procurement management plan’s vendor management practices using repository insights.

rows.stem: An engineering firm is delivering a large-scale infrastructure project funded by a public client. Midway through execution, the project manager notices significant cost and schedule variances that exc
rows.correctLabel: D. Submit a formal change request to adjust the baseline budget and timelines using updated estimates.

rows.stem: A project team is working during the project's early stages to identify potential risks for inclusion in the project management plan. They discover that stakeholders have many concerns that have not b
rows.correctLabel: B. Follow a continuous stakeholder identification strategy to avoid missing risks as they emerge.

rows.stem: A project manager is tracking a project’s progress toward meeting the organization’s strategic goals and business objectives. The key performance indicators (KPIs) show that the project is not meeting
rows.correctLabel: A. Tailor stakeholder engagement strategies to match their expertise related to resource management.

rows.stem: A regional hospital system is preparing for a merger with another healthcare provider. Management wants to take a structured approach to understanding staff attitudes and behaviors that could influenc
rows.correctLabel: A. Review the resource management plan and artifacts on standard operating procedures.

rows.stem: A financial services company is funding a project to develop a new software application. During planning, the project manager defines escalation thresholds to ensure performance stays within acceptabl
rows.correctLabel: B. Collaborate with key stakeholders to establish measurable key performance indicators (KPIs) and acceptable defect rates.

rows.stem: An engineering company is outsourcing building operations. Senior management wants to understand how team dynamics could affect prioritization and governance. The project manager has collected survey
rows.correctLabel: B. Apply cultural and political awareness tools to evaluate decision‑making influences.

rows.stem: A market research initiative is being launched using an agile approach to test demand for a new product. The team needs to define success metrics, but marketing managers are divided between customer e
rows.correctLabel: B. Define qualified recruit rate, time to insight, and interview completion predictability and review them at cadence points.

rows.stem: An entrepreneur has initiated a software development project. During development, unforeseen political risks arise, causing an urgent need to expedite the project. The sponsor has requested immediate
rows.correctLabel: A. Conduct a trend analysis using project performance data to forecast timeline impacts and identify patterns.

rows.stem: A project manager is leading a project that is 70% complete. The development team has been following the project management plan and the scope defined in the project charter. During a product review m
rows.correctLabel: A. Review the requested changes with the team and initiate the formal change control process if needed.

rows.stem: During the execution of a development project, a team member informs the project manager that the operating system installation will be delayed because the supplier has postponed a critical upgrade. T
rows.correctLabel: A. Assess how the delay impacts the project schedule and deliverables.

rows.stem: A project manager is leading a product development project in its fifth sprint. The product is due for release next quarter. The company is unexpectedly acquired by a competitor who already has a simi
rows.correctLabel: C. Seek support from the project sponsor and initiate appropriate change management procedures.

rows.stem: A key vendor providing specialized API integration components for an agile project is two weeks behind schedule due to unexpected security compliance issues. This delay impacts eight user stories acro
rows.correctLabel: A. Perform a thorough impact analysis of all resolution options, including costs and implications.

rows.stem: An agile team has established a comprehensive risk register during project initiation. The team has completed three sprints of development and is encountering evolving technical challenges and shiftin
rows.correctLabel: B. Schedule regular risk review meetings with the team and stakeholders to reevaluate impact and likelihood.
